# 🗄️ Database Schema (NoSQL)

**Primary DB:** Cloud Firestore (GCP)
**Auth Provider:** Firebase Authentication

*Reasoning:* Firestore pairs natively with Firebase Auth. This allows us to offload password management, security, and Google OAuth flows completely from our FastAPI backend to the Frontend, drastically speeding up the MVP without sacrificing "Pro" quality.

---

## Collection: `users`
**Document ID:** (String) Matches the `Firebase UID` generated by Firebase Auth.

```typescript
// NoSQL Document Structure
{
  "email": "hero@tuberpg.com",
  "username": "",                     // TODO: Add unique username logic
  "character_path": "backend_knight", // The chosen "Class" (e.g., 'data_oracle', 'devops_warlock')
  
  // Stats
  "current_hp": 100,                  // Drops on wrong answers. Max 0.
  "max_hp": 100,                      // Increases when leveling up
  "level": 1,                         // RPG Level
  
  // Progression
  "skills": [],                       // Array of unlocked abilities or "potions"
  "unlocked_nodes": ["video_intro_1"] // Array of YouTube video IDs the user can click on the map
}
```

*Note on NoSQL flexibility:* Since we are using Firestore, we don't need rigid migrations. If next week we decide the player needs an `inventory` array, we simply start saving it to the document. The Pydantic model in FastAPI will handle optional fields gracefully.
